title: viola × bavarica
intro: Viola × bavarica, also known as Bavarian pansy or sweet violet, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the genus Viola, characterized by its fragrant, heart-shaped leaves and delicate purple flowers. Traditionally, Viola × bavarica has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Viola × bavarica include its ability to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce stress. Viola × bavarica can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
